summary:
The discovery was made by Frederick Sanger who determined both the structure and amino acid sequence of insulin along with noting interspecies variations; he received his Nobel Prize in Chemistry for this groundbreaking research in 1958.
To summarize only the key clinical point from Agent2’s reasoning:

Frederick Sanger identified the structure and amino acid sequence of insulin, including interspecies differences, earning a Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 1958.
